behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between multiple local modules (three source clients + aggregation/orchestration + summary-input builder). Unit is too low because a single function test can miss wiring/merge omissions. System is not required: real network calls are not needed to catch this; in-process fakes for the three sources can reproduce missing-handoff bugs. Agentic is not required because this behavior validates pre-generation payload completeness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real aggregation/orchestration modules with three stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields. Execute one report build run and assert the exact payload passed into summary generation contains all three source sections/keys (including source identity), failing if any section is dropped or overwritten during module handoff.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestrator continuation -> section-level placeholder). Unit is too low because the real failure is often in inter-module control flow/state handoff. System is rejected because real OS/network conditions are not necessary; a failing in-process adapter/stub reproduces the failure. Agentic is rejected because behavior text targets fault-tolerance and placeholder insertion, not quality of model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source adapters returning normal data and one adapter raising an availability error. Assert process completes (no abort), output report contains a `data unavailable` marker for only the failed section, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated narrative quality/content, i.e., what the real model decides to say about numeric/key facts. Deterministic stubs would hide hallucination, omission, or misstatement failures (deterministic-assertion trap). Workflow is not required because this behavior concerns a single agentic generation step, not multiple agentic decisions chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key facts and run the real summary-generation model. Score outputs against factual checks (e.g., required key metrics/issues/deploy status must be correctly stated, no contradictory values). Repeat across multiple runs/prompts to capture nondeterministic misrepresentation rates and fail if accuracy threshold is not met.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external infrastructure: networked data sources/email transport and actual inbox delivery semantics. In-process mocks can pass while missing real SMTP/API auth, formatting, routing, or deliverability issues (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic is not the minimum here because the assertion is delivery-to-inbox end-to-end, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled pipeline end-to-end in a staging environment against real reachable endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify message arrival in the configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body/attachment structure and trace IDs confirming data-collection-to-delivery path.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local state handoff across components/runs: send failure handling -> durable local persistence -> subsequent-run retry logic. Unit is too low because bugs often occur at module boundaries and persisted-state coordination. System was considered but rejected: behavior can be reproduced with a deterministic failing mailer stub plus real local filesystem in-process; no real network outage simulation is strictly required for minimum detection.
behaviors.test_description: Run cycle 1 with email client stubbed to fail and real temp filesystem persistence enabled; assert report artifact is written with retry metadata. Run cycle 2 with email client healthy; assert persisted artifact is picked up, delivered once, and marked/cleaned to prevent duplicate retries.
